title: Use of Wanted
tagline: Past की इच्छाएँ बताना सीखो — 'Wanted' का master बनो!
summary: Day 12 में आप सीखेंगे 'Wanted' का सही उपयोग — यानी past में क्या चाहते थे, यह English में कैसे कहते हैं। 'Wanted' 'want' का past tense है और सभी subjects के साथ एक ही form में use होता है। आप सीखेंगे positive statements (I wanted to go), negative forms (I did not want to go), और questions (Did you want to go?). यह Day 11 के 'want/wants' का natural extension है।
hindiSummary: Day 11 में आपने 'want' और 'wants' का present tense सीखा। आज Day 12 में हम उसी structure का past tense सीखेंगे — 'wanted'। Main formula: Subject + wanted + to + verb या Subject + wanted + noun। Negative: Subject + did not want + to + verb। Question: Did + subject + want + to + verb? याद रखें — 'wanted' सभी subjects के लिए same है!
whatYouWillLearn: Formula 1: Subject + wanted + to + base verb (किसी काम की past में इच्छा)
Formula 2: Subject + wanted + noun (किसी चीज़ की past में इच्छा)
Negative: Subject + did not (didn't) + want + to + verb
Question: Did + subject + want + to + verb?
All subjects (I/he/she/we/they/you) use the SAME form 'wanted'
Common mistake: 'Did + subject + wanted' (WRONG) vs 'Did + subject + want' (RIGHT)
Polite expression: 'I just wanted to say...' / 'I wanted to inform you...'
Indirect speech: want → wanted in reported speech
Contrast with Day 11: want/wants (present) vs wanted (past)

wantedCheatSheet.positiveStatement: Subject + wanted + to + verb  |  Subject + wanted + noun
wantedCheatSheet.negativeStatement: Subject + did not / didn't + want + to + verb  |  did not want + noun
wantedCheatSheet.questionForm: Did + subject + want + to + verb?  |  Did + subject + want + noun?
wantedCheatSheet.allSubjects: I wanted / He wanted / She wanted / We wanted / They wanted / You wanted
wantedCheatSheet.commonMistake1: ❌ Did he WANTED? → ✅ Did he WANT?
wantedCheatSheet.commonMistake2: ❌ I didn't WANTED → ✅ I didn't WANT
wantedCheatSheet.commonMistake3: ❌ I wanted GO → ✅ I wanted TO GO
wantedCheatSheet.politeExpression: I just wanted to say... / I wanted to inform you... / She said she wanted to...
wantedCheatSheet.comparisonWithDay11: Present: I want tea (अभी) | Past: I wanted tea (तब)

motivationalQuote: Every great achievement started as something someone 'wanted to do'. Now is your time to use 'wanted' with confidence!

practiceTarget.writingTarget: Write a diary entry using 'wanted to' with all three forms (positive, negative, question)
